Toppings: Spicy louisiana style chicken, blue cheese, mozzarella
Texture Descriptor: Chewy, a little airy, still flat
Crust Brand?: Unknown, I asked and they said it was made in house but I doubt it
Service Notes: Fine, a little pushy and rushy, all foreign?

Toppings: house made vodka sauce, red sauce, garlic parsley pesto, sesame seed crust
Texture Descriptor: Crispy on the edges, sesame oil taste present, airy, like real bread, a little like a focaccia or flatbread, I wanted to eat the whole thing
Crust Brand?: Made in house, deep dish style
Service Notes: Fine, the server didn't have good answers to our questions, didn't know the difference between the different sauces, a little loud, cold

Texture Descriptor: Crisp and thin, sharp on the edges
Crust Brand?: Unknown, standard gf crust, think and hard

Toppings: Chicken, broccoli, garlic & basil pesto (& other morsels for uplifting gormandizers)
Texture Descriptor: Hard, chewy, difficult the pull apart
Service Notes: Really nice people, not great pizza

Texture Descriptor: Similar to Fumo, a little airy, but still crispy
Hand made crust, good and airy but not crispy
Service Notes: Loud location, pizza had all the right elements but just wasn't crispy enough to be great.
not great for eating in, you can ask for it to be extra crispy, just need to wait, 10" pie

Toppings: Smoked bacon, hot sausage, sautéed mushroom, hot cherry peppers, fresh garlic & basil.
Texture Descriptor: Chewy, tapioca, cheesy, Against the Grain, just not cooked well enough
Crust Brand?: Against the Grain, not very well cooked
Service Notes: Cute place, ridiculous price for the crust, many of the pies didn;t have sauce, and this crust needed it

Texture Descriptor: Chewy, light, a little wet with sauce, very italian
Crust Brand?: Made in house, the owner will come over and talk to you and tell you about it. He's very proud (and should be). Webiste mentions:  rice, psyilllium husk, pea protein and quinoa
Service Notes: Close to me in the UWS, which is a bonus. You'll usually need a reservation and it's cash only. The owner must be gluten free or know someone who is, because he makes a lot of things in house: gnocci, burger buns, pizza crusts. Intimate inside (aka close), but fast service. Cash only. Very itialian style pizza. not sliced, served with a knife. Ask for the chili oil

Texture Descriptor: Chewy, light, a little wet with sauce. Similar to Celeste. Roman style. No new haven style char. Held together well enough to get a bend.
Crust Brand?: Made in house, similar to celeste in style and taste. thin, light, chewy, not charred on bottom
Service Notes: They have half off happy hour pizza, which does not apply to the gluten free pizzas. why is that? I don't know. Their menu says "all pizzas half off." If I were a lawyer, or perhaps larry david, I'd put up a fight about this. Unforutantely, I'm neither. We had overall pretty bad service even though we were sitting at the bar. Cool place, good vibes, probably just got unlucky. A bit difficult to get our order in even when it was mostly empty. We came here before going to the Angelika, and I'd do it again, and I think I'll enjoy the experience more. I'm starting to think Roman style pizza means wet. This does come presliced.

Texture Descriptor: Airy, maybe too thick, well charred
Service Notes: This guy can do it all. Just look at the photos

Toppings: Mozzarella, Mushrooms, Italian Sweet Sausage, Basil
Texture Descriptor: Finally a bit of char to the crust after many wet pizzas. Home made, held its consistency together, tasted good. 5 stars
Service Notes: Despite how good the pizza was, the vibe inside was horrendous. reminded me of an ihop or dennys. went here in route to MoMA. Worth it for a test visit, but I won't be going back just because I hated it so much inside for no easily explainable reason. You may feel otherwise.
